diagrams.title: Prim's Algorithm Cut Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the cut property where vertices are partitioned into visited (MST) and unvisited sets, with minimum edge crossing highlighted
elements: Edges crossing cut (all shown, minimum one highlighted
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the cut property - the theoretical foundation of why Prim's greedy choice is always safe

diagrams.title: Prim's Algorithm Step-by-Step Growth
diagrams.description: Shows progressive growth of MST from single vertex, adding one vertex at a time through minimum weight edges
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how Prim's algorithm incrementally builds the MST one vertex at a time

diagrams.title: Comparison: Prim's vs Kruskal's
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing how Prim grows one tree vs Kruskal's edge-by-edge forest merging
elements: Prim's: single tree growing from root
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the fundamental difference between these two greedy MST algorithms
